user_query:
I’m maintaining a small local archive of oral-history transcripts for Italian-American community figures and need a lightweight event-triggered automation that keeps a normalized dataset up to date whenever I add new transcripts. Please implement a local folder watcher and extraction pipeline that: 1) Watches input/incoming/ for .txt or .html transcripts and, on new or modified files, extracts structured metadata into output/interviews.jsonl. Each line in the JSONL should be a single interview record with these fields: - id: filename without extension (e.g., giovanna_russo) - interviewee_name: string - interview_date: string in YYYY-MM-DD - birthplace: string - occupation: string - contributions: array of strings (split on semicolons) - summary: string - source_path: relative path to the source file 2) The extractor must handle the three patterns present in the provided inputs: - Plain-text labels (input/incoming/giovanna_russo.txt): lines starting with “Interviewee:”, “Interview Date:”, “Birthplace:”, “Occupation:”, “Key Contributions:”, and “Summary:”. - HTML with tags (input/incoming/angelo_martelli.html): , , , , ; summary text is in

. - HTML definition list (input/staged/roberto_colombo.html): a

with
/
for Interviewee, Interview Date, Birthplace, Occupation, Key Contributions; summary text is in

. 3) Provide two commands: - A long-running watcher command I can start (e.g., scripts/watch_and_extract) that monitors input/incoming/ and updates output/interviews.jsonl as events occur, appending/updating records by id. - A one-off full rebuild command (e.g., scripts/rebuild) that reprocesses all files in input/incoming/ and overwrites output/interviews.jsonl deterministically. 4) Logging: append an entry to output/run.log for every processed file with ISO timestamp, action (added/updated), and id. 5) Validation: add a test/validation command (e.g., scripts/validate) that: - Checks output/interviews.jsonl has exactly one record per transcript file currently present in input/incoming/. - Verifies required fields are non-empty and interview_date is in YYYY-MM-DD. - Writes a human-readable summary to output/validation_report.txt and exits non-zero on failure. Simulation to demonstrate the event trigger: - Start your watcher. - Simulate a new arrival by moving input/staged/roberto_colombo.html into input/incoming/ while the watcher is running, and let it process. - Then run the rebuild command once to ensure idempotent output. - Finally, run the validation command to generate output/validation_report.txt. Deliverables I will check locally: - output/interviews.jsonl containing one record for each transcript (including roberto_colombo after the simulated move) with the specified fields and normalized date format. - output/run.log showing processed filenames/ids with timestamps and actions. - output/validation_report.txt from the validation command indicating success. Use any language or tools you prefer as long as I can run them with standard shell commands in a normal local workspace. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
